agency: NEW YORK CITY CAMPAIGN FINANCE BOARD
application_description: This system stores participating candidates' financial information as reported to the Campaign Finance Board by the campaign committees. This system is used to calculate public fund payments to qualifying candidates, monitor compliance with the Program's contribution and expenditure limits, public disclosure and other requirements, and assist the Board in conducting desk and field audits of candidates' campaigns. For public disclosure purposes, CFIS enables the Board to produce alphabetized reports of the campaign financial data candidates report to the Board. For hundreds of participating candidates for the 1989 and 1991 elections, CFIS was used to prepare the following public disclosure reports which are available at the Board's Offices: 1) by contributor Name, 2)by contributor Employer Name, 3)by Intermediary Name, and 4)by Payee/vendor name.
database_contents: 1) CONTRIBUTIONS: a) contributor name and address, b) name and address of contributor's employer, if supplied, c) intermediary name and address, if supplied, d) date the contribution was received and the statement it was reported on, e) amount of contribution and, f) amount of contribution claimed by candidate as "matchable" or for purposes of meeting the threshold. 2) EXPENDITURES: a) payee name, b) expenditure date and c) amount and purpose of the expenditure. AVAILABILITY NOTE: Available to the public. Please note that from election to election the fields and organization of the database may vary, as a result of changes in the campaign Finance Act, the campaign Finance program's reporting requirements, and enhancements to the computer system.

application_description: The Geosupport Information system (GIS) is an integrated computer system of software and files that supports many of the geographic processing needs common to New York City agencies ' applications. It is accessed directly by other City computer applications on an IBM mainframe. The data files in GIS are, specially structured to be accessed directly only through the GIS software. However, the data are accessible in the form of printed address directories, computer generated printed maps and a microcomputer-usable format on floppy disks that are for sale to the public.
database_contents: The following are available for purchase through the Department of City Planning Bookstore, 22 Reade st., New York, NY 10007. Tele: [212] 720-3667. 1. Address Directories by Borough: A Borough Address Directory shows, for each street, the address range on each block face, and the corresponding 1980 census tract, 1990 census tract, Zip code, Community District, School District, Police Precinct, and Health Areas. There are also entries for alternative street names and some street name spelling variants. 2. Printed Computer Generated Maps: 1990 Census Tract maps are available as a citywide map at, a scale of 1 inch =4,000 feet and for each borough at 1 inch =2,000 feet. (Note: These maps depict the census geography only, not demographic data). 1990 Census Tract and Census Block maps are available at a scale of 1 inch =600 feet. A set of 44 sectional maps shows census tract boundaries and numbers, census block numbers, and street names. (Note: These maps depict the census geography only, not demographic data). City-owned and leased property from the 1991 Atlas of City Property: These 22 maps show all property that is owned and leased by the city of New York at a scale of 1 inch = 800 feet. Symbols depict properties hy use codes defined in the legend with an abbreviated description of the property. Tax Block and Lot Maps are available as a booklet for each community district (except Staten Island). These publications contain detailed maps at a scale of 1 inch = 250 feet showing block outlines and number, and tax lot outlines and numbers. 3. cartographic Files on'Floppy Disks--Bytes of the Big Apple (TM). Tax Block Files in DXF (TM) format for use with microcomputers running DOS. These have tax block outlines, tax block numbers and street names. They can he used with any CAD or mapping software that can import DXF files. Tax Block files in Illustrator 88 (TM) format for use on Apple MacIntosh (R) computers. 'These files have tax block outlines, tax block numbers and street names. They can only he used with Adobe Illustrator 88 (TM) •• software. LION single Line.street Base Map Files in Maplnfo ~ format for use with DOS operating system. These files have the city'S street geography on seven floppy disks. The files contain street names, address ranges and geographic locations expressed in New York East State Plane Coordinates. These files can only he used with MapInfo (R) •••software. • Bytes of the Big Apple is a trademark of the Department of City Planning • •• Illustrator 88 is a trademark of Adobe System, Inc. •••MapInfo is a registered trademark of the MapInfo corporation.

application_description: LUMIS tracks ULURPand non-ULURP applications from receipt hy the Department of City Planning through final review hy the City Planning Commission, City Council and the Mayor.

The data resides in a mainframe database system.

application_description: MISLAND is a report generation system used to obtain recent and historical land use, housing and population data about the City'S neighborhoods. Users may choose from over thirty standard report formats. Most of the data originates from other city agencies and is processed by City Planning for the MISLAND system.
database_contents: Vacant structures; residential structures' vacancy rates; land use; property transactions; real estate tax arrearage; city owned and leased property inclUding property name and address, use, and responsible agency; residential construction, conversions and demolitions; land use acreage; building floor area; housing unit changes; assessed values; property market values; property address and location; property owner's name; building and lot dimensions; property sales aggregations; and selected 1970, 1980 and 1990 Census information -including housing, demographic, social and economic Characteristics.
method_of_access: customized report(s) will be produced. Users must request the report name and list their geography either by Community District(s), census tract(s), or tax block(s) depending upon the report requested. Users may request a special report title and selection criteria such as year(s) of data, building classes, and summary criteria depending upon the requested report's available options. The charge per report is $25 for the first 100 pages and $10 for each additional 100 pages. MISLAND User's Manuals are available by calling (212) 720-3587.

application_description: The Housing Database is a centralized and standardized source of information on city, state, federal and quasi-governmental housing and insurance programs. Its a historical file and contains data relevant to the housing's geographic location and physical characteristics; and information pertaining to the specific housing programs that fund or have funded the site. The data originates from other city, state and federal agencies and are processed by Cits Planning for the Housing Database.
database_contents: Address; community district; tax block and lot; census tract and block; project name; number of stories; units; housing program name; type of program; program status; milestone dates; and government and private monies. The data resides in a mainframe database system.
method_of_access: Upon written request, a Master Housing Database community District Report will be produced for each community district that mas been requested. This report lists all buildings in the Housing Database by taxblock and lot, and inclUdes the building address, current number of units, and all housing programs that have or are currently funding the building and their most recent status and status date. Each report will contain information on a single community district. The cost is $25.00 for the first 100 pages, and $10 for each additional 100 pages of a report.

application_description: The Selected School Enrollment projections File contains annual five- and ten-year public school enrollment projections for kindergarten grade through 9th grade by school district, and by borough for high schools. application_description: and DATABASE CONTENTS Background: The Charter requires that the head of each city agency submit to the mayor each year an up-to-date inventory, assessment, and schedule of maintenance activities and necessary repairs for that agency's portion of the city's capital plant. Process: j The mayor must then transmit to the city council an estimate of the amounts needed, by agency, for the required maintenance and repair activities. The condition assessments, maintenance schedules and estimates must he prepared and certified by registered engineers and,architects. Finally, the mayor shall include in the bUdget message an itemized statement detailing any differences between the amounts indicated as required and the amounts appropriated for capital plant maintenance as well as the amounts actually expended. summary: The City's physical plant is defined by the Charter to include all assets or systems with a replacement value of at least $10 million and useful life of at least ten years. Even with these restrictions, the individual assets identified and appraised in the first phase of the project number approximately 2500 and include asset types such as parks, schools, office buildings, police precincts, prominent bridges, bulkheads, and ferryboats. The city's approximately 5700 miles of streets are also included in this project. Assets leased to the Transit Authority, the NYC water Finance Authority and to other public benefit corporations are excluded from the Charter reporting requirements. The City mas chosen to include assets of the Health and Hospital corporation. This application also includes the inventory assessments, maintenance and repair activities required by the Charter.

and DATABASE CONTENTS The capital Information systems, CAPIS, is software for developing and maintaining the City's capital commitment Plan, the Request, Executive and Adopted BUdgets and the three-year Capital Program. Bi-annually CAPIS produces the City's Ten Year capital Plan which sets forth the city's programmatic goals for capital improvement. CAPIS is a CICS (customer' Information and Control System) online system. Application programs are written in command level COBOL, and many call modules or routines are assembler macros. within CAPIS there are two subsystems, the Design, Award and Construction system (DACS,) and the Ten Year Plan. CAPIS includes approximately 100 on-line screens and associated programs, which provide inquiry and update functions to users. In addition to the online system there are 25 to 30 batch programs which support CAPIS and DACS. In CAPIS the basic entity is the CAPIS project which contain location, scope, and schedule information along with forecasts of capital commitments from all funding sources. within each CAPIS project commitment forecast are details called occurrences which represent either a complete capital contract with amount and commitment date or a part of a capital contract. Each detail consists of codes which describe the type of work, the funding (budget line), a date, and the funded amount (City Exempt, Non-exempt, Federal, State or Private. Each CAPIS project mas an approval status or Financial status Code (FSC). A project mas approved status if OMB changes the four character FSC to an "approved" FSC starting with "EX" or "PL". When a project is approved CAPIS generates an approved detail equal to the agency's forecast, OMB may elect to change the timing or the amount of the approved detail, thus a CAPIS project may have a variance between the agency's estimates and OMB's approved estimates. CAPIS contains approximately 10,800 approved projects of which 4,500 are active in the current four year program. These active projects contain about 9,000 details or occurrences.

application_name: COKMUNXTY BOARD REPORTS 1981 Register of Community Board Requests 1986 Various Geographic Capital Reports
application_description: OMB's Office of community Board Relations publishes various bUdget documents for release as part of the preliminary, Executive and Adopted BUdgets. The capital bUdget documents fulfill the requirements of Charter section 214a3.
database_contents: 1. The Geographic Report for the Capital BUdget contains cost estimates of site specific capital projects for the next fiscal year and the three succeeding years sorted by community district. Appropriations-to-date and expenditures-to-date are included for each project. Also listed are borough and citywide projects. 2. The Geographic capital commitment Plan is organized by community district and shows the capital appropriations and commitments for the current fiscal year and the next thee years for all active capital bUdget projects, and project implementation schedules which give the best estimate of the completion time for activities such as scope, design and construction. 3. The Geographic capital Detail Data Report shows each CAPXS project (capital project) within a bUdget line. Sorted by community district, available information includes bUdget costs, an implementation schedule, project location and a scope summary. For projects supported by community boards, this report crosswalks the bUdget lines and CAPXS detail with the community board tracking code. 4. The Register of Community Board BUdget Requests publishes the responses to the capital and expense bUdget requests submitted by the community boards. Agency, OMB and Adopted BUdget funding recommendations are presented for the successive stages of the bUdget process (Charter section B and 212).

application_description: and DATABASE CONTENTS In accordance with section 106(b) of the City Charter, the Mayor shall establish Quarterly spending Allotments and aggregate position and salary limits for each unit of appropriation within an agency. TheQuarterlyAllocation System (QAS) is used to'plan and monitor monthly spending by agency. Expenditure plans are maintained by Personal Service (e.g. salaries, overtime, etc.) and Other Than Personal Services (e.g. equipment, rent, etc.). Agencies are required to remain within their spending plan hy unit of appropriation and funding source. The QAS supports various fiscal monitoring exercises which include cashflow, variance reporting and surplus/needs exercises. Expenditure forecasts for the City's cashflow are prepared each month. Variance reports on dollars and employee headcount are issued monthly to the agencies with a comparison between their actuals and plan. Any overspending/underspending that mas occurred must be explained by the agency. This system provides early warnings on overspending and enables an agency to take corrective action. The surplus/needs analysis projects variances for the remainder of the current fiscal year for each agency, and surpluses and needs are balanced out across agencies to ensure that on an aggregate basis the City is within budget. The QAS data supports development of the Four Year Financial Plan.
